description: For the chronically procrastinating, currently-failing, GPA-on-life-support crowd.

roasts.template: Your GPA and your future are both on academic probation. The doctors are discussing when to pull the plug.
You don't procrastinate. You just prefer doing things in a future you haven't ruined yet.
Your study group is just you, your phone, and denial. And your phone is winning.
You failed so hard the curve gave up on you. The curve is unionized now.
Your essay has the same structure as a house of cards — impressive on paper, collapses on contact.
You don't need a degree. You need a participation certificate and a long, honest conversation.
Your transcript reads like a cry for help written in course codes. CRY101: Required.
You study the way I check my phone — with no real commitment to the outcome.
Your professor has you in their 'students to avoid remembering' folder. It's a thick folder.
You don't have senioritis. You have junior regret and a senior workload.
The only A you're getting is in attendance. And even that's iffy.
You treat deadlines like suggestions and suggestions like rules. Both are wrong.
You majored in 'figuring it out later.' Spoiler: you didn't.
You didn't drop out. You were expelled by reality. And reality doesn't accept appeals.
Your attendance is the only thing passing. Like a lighthouse in a sea of F's. The lighthouse union has filed a complaint.
You wrote a 12-page essay on why you couldn't write a 12-page essay. The professor gave you an A for honesty. They were being sarcastic. Mostly.
You treat the syllabus like a suggestion document, the rubric like a rumor, and the deadline like a hoax. The academic integrity office has your photo on a dartboard.
Your major says 'undeclared' but your transcript says 'unmotivated.' And the bar is on the floor. The bar is also on fire.
You have a 4.0 in sleeping through 8ams and a 0.0 in showing up. The math is overwhelming. So is your sleep schedule.
You treat office hours like they don't exist. The professor has accepted it. They have other students. Better students. They send holiday cards.
Your parents brag about your 'potential.' Potential doesn't have a GPA. Yours wouldn't, anyway. Your parents' friends know this.
You study abroad — abroad from your responsibilities, that is. The trip is endless. The passport is full of excuses.
You cite Wikipedia in academic papers. Wikipedia cited you back, asking you to stop. Publicly. The article is in the edit history.
You don't have a thesis statement. You have a thesis suggestion. And you're not following it. Or any other instruction. Or any other suggestion.
You treat the group project like it's group punishment. Your partners agree. They have a group chat without you. You're not in the read receipt.
You graduated. The only thing that graduated is your student debt. It has its own zip code now. The IRS sends you holiday cards.
